behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality (thread-grounded specificity vs generic drafting). A mocked or deterministic stub can pass while the real model still produces vague replies, so lower-level tests can miss the real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real drafting model on a set of real/synthetic email threads with identifiable details (names, dates, asks). Assert the draft includes thread-specific references (per rubric) and fails when outputs are generic across distinct threads.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: If contradiction detection uses model judgment, real failures are classification-quality failures that mocks hide. You need the real checker behavior against contradictory claims to catch misses/false negatives.
behaviors.test_description: Feed drafts containing known contradictions to KB facts into the real consistency-check step and assert contradictions are flagged with correct claim spans/reasons. Include near-miss cases to ensure it is not only keyword matching.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is hallucination/abstention behavior of the real model. Integration tests can verify control flow, but only real model runs reveal whether it fabricates facts instead of disclaiming.
behaviors.test_description: Use threads requiring external facts while providing an empty/irrelevant KB to the real generation path. Assert the draft includes the required disclaimer and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local state/hand-off correctness across workflow modules (drafting -> approval gate -> sender). A single-unit test can miss wiring bugs where send is triggered before approval.
behaviors.test_description: In an integrated local test (handler/service/repo/sender adapter), create a draft, do not issue approval, and assert no send action is emitted. Then issue explicit approval and assert exactly one send is emitted.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: This validates a multi-step pipeline including real agentic steps in sequence (generation and factual check) plus gating. Failures can emerge only from cross-step interactions and cumulative model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run on a real email thread: ingest thread, run real draft generation, run real fact check against KB, verify approval UI/state is produced, and confirm send remains blocked until approval. Validate intermediate artifacts across steps.
